tasks.title: Repository State Analysis & Inventory
tasks.description: Create a complete inventory of the repository, capturing file metadata, dependencies, and entry points to establish a baseline before reorganization.
tasks.details:
Implement a comprehensive repository analysis tool that:

1. Traverses the entire repository structure using `os.walk()` or `pathlib`
2. Calculates MD5 hash for each file to identify duplicates and track changes
3. Extracts file metadata (size, last modified, permissions)
4. Builds a dependency graph by parsing Python imports and JavaScript/TypeScript imports
5. Identifies critical entry points (main.py, app.py, etc.)
6. Detects naming inconsistencies (FreeAgentics vs. cogniticnet)
7. Stores the complete inventory in a structured JSON format

Implementation should use AST parsing for Python files to accurately extract imports rather than regex. For JavaScript/TypeScript, use a proper parser like `esprima` or `babel-parser`.

The output should include:
- Complete file list with metadata
- Dependency graph (what imports what)
- Entry points list
- Potential duplicates
- Naming inconsistencies

This inventory will serve as the foundation for safe reorganization.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify inventory completeness by comparing file count with `find . -type f | wc -l`
2. Validate MD5 hash calculation by comparing with system `md5sum` tool
3. Test dependency extraction with known import patterns
4. Verify entry point detection with manual review
5. Test on sample repositories with known structure
6. Ensure the tool handles edge cases like circular imports and dynamic imports

subtasks.title: Repository Traversal Implementation
subtasks.description: Develop a robust module to recursively traverse the repository file system, efficiently handling large codebases and various directory structures. Ensure modularity for easy integration and testability.
subtasks.details:
Follow best practices for file system access, handle symlinks, hidden files, and permission errors gracefully. Provide hooks for filtering files by type or pattern as recommended by expert committee guidance.

subtasks.title: MD5 Hash Calculation for Files
subtasks.description: Implement functionality to compute MD5 hashes for all relevant files discovered during traversal, ensuring accuracy and performance.
subtasks.details:
Optimize for large files and parallel processing where feasible. Store hashes in a way that supports later validation and change detection. Ensure code is modular and testable.
<info added on 2025-06-20T07:50:23.697Z>
MD5 Hash Calculation Implementation completed successfully. The MD5Calculator class provides both synchronous and asynchronous hash calculation with automatic optimization based on file size. Large files (>100MB) use streaming with 64KB buffers while smaller files use in-memory processing. Parallel processing via ThreadPoolExecutor handles multiple files efficiently, scaling worker threads based on CPU cores.

The implementation includes comprehensive error handling for permission denied, file not found, and IO errors. Each file's hash calculation returns a status (SUCCESS, PERMISSION_DENIED, FILE_NOT_FOUND, ERROR) along with the hash value when successful. Built-in validation against system md5sum/md5 tools ensures accuracy.

Integration with the traversal module is achieved through FileInfo objects that now include hash values and calculation status. The module tracks detailed statistics including total files processed, success/failure counts, processing time, total bytes processed, and duplicate file detection.

Test results confirm successful hash calculation for various file types with proper error handling for inaccessible files. The implementation follows SOLID principles with clear separation of concerns, making it extensible for future hash algorithm additions.
</info added on 2025-06-20T07:50:23.697Z>

subtasks.title: Entry Point Detection
subtasks.description: Identify and catalog entry points (e.g., main scripts, CLI commands, web server initializers) in the repository for supported languages.
subtasks.details:
Use language conventions and configuration files (e.g., setup.py, package.json, Dockerfiles) to detect entry points. Ensure detection logic is extensible and well-documented.
<info added on 2025-06-20T08:06:14.382Z>
Successfully implemented comprehensive entry point detection system with multi-strategy approach combining filename patterns, content analysis, and framework-specific detection. Created entry_point_detector.py with advanced features including AST/regex parsing, criticality classification (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), and dependency graph integration. 

Analyzed 100 files from CogniticNet repository, detecting 19 entry points with HIGH overall risk level. Found 2 CRITICAL entry points (migrate-to-freeagentics.py with __main__ guard, requirements.txt) and 11 HIGH criticality points including configuration files and framework hooks. Average confidence score of 0.82 indicates very reliable detection.

Key capabilities include framework awareness for Django/Flask/FastAPI/Express/React/pytest patterns, content-based detection using AST and regex parsing, risk assessment with protection recommendations, and enhanced accuracy through dependency graph integration. Detection methods use confidence scoring for reliability assessment.

Critical findings include main application entry at migrate-to-freeagentics.py, essential configuration files (requirements.txt, package.json, pyproject.toml), and framework-specific entry points like jest.setup.js. System provides protection recommendations for each entry point type to ensure safe reorganization.
</info added on 2025-06-20T08:06:14.382Z>

subtasks.title: Naming Inconsistency Detection
subtasks.description: Analyze file, directory, and symbol names to detect inconsistencies or violations of naming conventions across the repository.
subtasks.details:
Implement checks for case sensitivity, snake_case vs camelCase, and other project-specific rules. Provide actionable reports and suggestions for remediation.
<info added on 2025-06-20T08:09:17.315Z>
Successfully implemented comprehensive naming analyzer following expert committee guidance from Kevlin Henney and Robert Martin.

Created scripts/repository_analysis/naming_analyzer.py with advanced naming convention detection capabilities including multi-context analysis for files, directories, Python/JS variables, functions, and classes. The analyzer features pattern detection and violation identification with severity classification, plus automatic convention inference from existing codebase patterns.

Key features include convention detection (automatically detects snake_case, camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case), language awareness respecting Python and JavaScript/TypeScript conventions, context sensitivity with different rules for various code elements, violation analysis with severity classification and suggested fixes, and pattern learning that adapts to project's existing conventions when dominant (>70% usage).

Testing on 150 files from CogniticNet repository revealed 971 naming violations (928 medium, 43 low priority), extensive directory naming inconsistencies, and file naming patterns requiring standardization. Key findings include system directories not following kebab-case, mixed file naming conventions, generally compliant code naming, and mostly medium-priority violations indicating manageable cleanup.

Generated recommendations include establishing documented naming conventions, using automated linters for enforcement, standardizing file naming to kebab-case, and focusing on project-specific files rather than system paths. Technical implementation uses pattern recognition with regex and AST analysis, confidence-based convention detection, and provides an extensible framework with detailed violation reporting and suggested fixes.
</info added on 2025-06-20T08:09:17.315Z>

tasks.title: Establish Test Baseline & CI Pipeline
tasks.description: Discover all existing tests, establish a baseline of test coverage, and implement a CI pipeline to ensure changes don't break functionality.
tasks.details:
1. Discover all test files in the repository using pattern matching:
   - Python: `test_*.py`, `*_test.py`
   - JavaScript: `*.spec.js`, `*.test.js`
   - End-to-end tests in dedicated directories

2. Categorize tests by type (unit, integration, e2e)

3. Set up test runners for each language/framework:
   - Python: pytest with coverage
   - JavaScript: Jest with coverage

4. Create a baseline report of:
   - Test count by category
   - Pass/fail status
   - Code coverage percentage
   - Test execution time

5. Implement GitHub Actions workflow for CI:
```yaml
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v4
        with:
          python-version: '3.10'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install pytest pytest-cov
      - name: Test with pytest
        run: pytest --cov=. --cov-report=xml
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
```

6. Add pre-commit hooks to run tests locally before commits

7. Create a test report dashboard for visibility
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify all tests are discovered by comparing with manual inventory
2. Ensure baseline metrics are accurately captured
3. Validate CI pipeline by introducing a passing test and a failing test
4. Test pre-commit hooks with various scenarios
5. Verify coverage reports match expected values

Framework detection identified pytest (34 files), unittest (5 files), jest (2 files), and 5 files with unknown frameworks. Test categorization revealed unit tests (36 files), e2e tests (9 files), and performance tests (1 file). Discovery system completed comprehensive analysis in 3.30 seconds.

Test type classification includes unit, integration, e2e, performance, smoke, and regression categories. Quality metrics detect assertions, mocks, and fixtures. Configuration discovery located pytest configs (2 files) and jest configs (1530 files).

Technical implementation features AST-based analysis for Python test extraction, content pattern matching for framework-specific detection, confidence scoring for reliability assessment, extensible architecture for new languages/frameworks, and robust error handling with detailed reporting.

Key findings indicate need for test framework standardization due to multiple frameworks detected. Integration test coverage gaps identified for component interactions. Framework configurations successfully catalogued for CI pipeline setup.

Test discovery foundation provides comprehensive baseline for categorization and tagging phase. All test assets identified and catalogued, ready for systematic organization and CI pipeline integration.

tasks.title: Design New Directory Structure
tasks.description: Create a domain-driven directory structure that properly separates concerns and follows clean architecture principles.
tasks.details:
Design a new directory structure following domain-driven design and clean architecture principles:

```
freeagentics/
├── agents/                # Core domain - Agent definitions
│   ├── explorer/
│   ├── merchant/
│   ├── scholar/
│   └── base/
├── inference/             # Core domain - Active Inference
│   ├── engine/
│   ├── gnn/
│   └── llm/
├── coalitions/            # Core domain - Coalition formation
│   ├── formation/
│   ├── contracts/
│   └── deployment/
├── world/                 # Core domain - Environment
│   ├── grid/
│   ├── resources/
│   └── physics/
├── api/                   # Interface layer
│   ├── rest/
│   ├── websocket/
│   └── graphql/
├── web/                   # Interface layer - Frontend
│   ├── src/
│   └── public/
├── infrastructure/        # Infrastructure layer
│   ├── docker/
│   ├── kubernetes/
│   └── terraform/
├── config/                # Configuration
│   ├── environments/
│   ├── database/
│   └── logging/
├── data/                  # Data assets
├── scripts/               # Automation
├── tests/                 # Test suites
└── docs/                  # Documentation
```

Key principles:
1. Domain-first organization (agents, inference, coalitions, world)
2. Clear separation of interface, domain, and infrastructure
3. Configuration separate from code
4. Tests mirror source structure
5. Documentation as first-class citizen

Create an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) documenting:
- Rationale for structure
- Dependency rules (what can import what)
- Naming conventions
- File organization principles

The structure should support:
- Active Inference implementation
- Agent creation and management
- Coalition formation
- H3 spatial integration
- LLM-informed belief updates
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Review structure with team members for feedback
2. Validate against domain model
3. Check that structure accommodates all existing functionality
4. Ensure structure follows clean architecture principles
5. Verify naming consistency throughout

tasks.title: Implement File Movement Plan
tasks.description: Create and execute a safe plan for moving files to the new structure while preserving git history and maintaining functionality.
tasks.details:
Create a detailed file movement plan that:

1. Maps each file from current location to target location in new structure
2. Calculates correct movement order based on dependency graph
3. Updates import statements in all affected files
4. Preserves git history using `git mv`

Implementation steps:

```python
def plan_file_movements():
    # Load file inventory and dependency graph from Task 1
    inventory = load_inventory()
    dependencies = load_dependency_graph()
    
    # Create mapping of current to target locations
    file_mapping = {}
    for file_path in inventory:
        target_path = determine_target_location(file_path)
        if target_path != file_path:
            file_mapping[file_path] = target_path
    
    # Sort files by dependency order (least dependencies first)
    sorted_files = topological_sort(file_mapping.keys(), dependencies)
    
    # Generate movement commands
    commands = []
    for file in sorted_files:
        if file in file_mapping:
            commands.append({
                'source': file,
                'target': file_mapping[file],
                'git_command': f'git mv {file} {file_mapping[file]}',
                'affected_files': find_files_importing(file)
            })
    
    return commands
```

Execute the plan in batches:
1. Move a small batch of files (5-10)
2. Update import statements in affected files
3. Run tests to verify functionality
4. Commit changes
5. Repeat until all files are moved

For import updates, use AST manipulation rather than string replacement to avoid changing imports in strings or comments.

Include rollback procedures for each batch in case of test failures.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test the movement plan on a copy of the repository first
2. After each batch of moves, run the full test suite
3. Verify import statements are correctly updated
4. Check that git history is preserved using `git log --follow`
5. Validate that the build still works after all movements
6. Test rollback procedures on a sample batch

subtasks.title: File Mapping Generation
subtasks.description: Design and implement a comprehensive mapping of all files to be moved, including their current and target locations, metadata, and any special handling requirements. Ensure the mapping accounts for project structure, naming conventions, and future maintainability.
subtasks.details: Leverage automated tools and scripts to scan the codebase, generate a mapping file (e.g., JSON or CSV), and validate the mapping with stakeholders. Incorporate expert committee recommendations for clarity and extensibility.

subtasks.title: Dependency-Based Movement Ordering
subtasks.description: Analyze code dependencies to determine a safe and efficient order for moving files, minimizing breakage and circular dependencies. Prioritize moves that unblock others and reduce risk.
subtasks.details: Utilize static analysis tools to build a dependency graph. Apply topological sorting or similar algorithms to derive the optimal movement sequence, following best practices from Kent Beck and Robert Martin on incremental, safe change.

subtasks.title: Import Statement Updating
subtasks.description: Automate the updating of import statements and references throughout the codebase to reflect new file locations, ensuring code remains functional after each move.
subtasks.details: Develop or adapt scripts to parse and rewrite import paths. Include tests to verify correctness. Follow Martin Fowler’s guidance on behavior-preserving refactoring and incremental change.

subtasks.title: Git History Preservation
subtasks.description: Ensure that file moves and refactorings preserve git history for traceability and accountability, using git-native operations and best practices.
subtasks.details: Use 'git mv' and related commands to retain file history. Document procedures for reviewers. Validate history preservation with sample queries and expert committee sign-off.

subtasks.title: Batch Move/Test/Commit Cycles
subtasks.description: Implement a process for moving files in manageable batches, running automated tests after each batch, and committing only when tests pass. This minimizes disruption and enables rapid feedback.
subtasks.details: Define batch sizes based on complexity analysis. Automate the cycle: move files, update imports, run tests, and commit. Roll back and adjust as needed if failures occur. Document each batch for traceability.

subtasks.title: Rollback Procedure Implementation
subtasks.description: Develop robust rollback procedures to quickly revert changes in case of failures, ensuring minimal downtime and data integrity.
subtasks.details: Script and document rollback steps for each batch. Integrate with CI/CD pipelines. Test rollback scenarios to ensure reliability, following expert recommendations for safety nets and continuous improvement.

tasks.title: Implement Naming Consistency
tasks.description: Establish and enforce consistent naming conventions throughout the codebase for files, variables, classes, and functions.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive naming consistency across the repository:

1. Project name: Use 'FreeAgentics' for product name, 'freeagentics' for package/module names

2. File naming conventions:
   - Python files: kebab-case.py (e.g., belief-update.py)
   - TypeScript/JavaScript files: 
     - Components: PascalCase.tsx (e.g., AgentCreator.tsx)
     - Utilities: camelCase.ts (e.g., beliefUtils.ts)
   - Configuration files: kebab-case.yml (e.g., development.yml)
   - Documentation: kebab-case.md (e.g., getting-started.md)

3. Code naming conventions:
   - Python:
     - Classes: PascalCase (e.g., ExplorerAgent)
     - Functions/methods: snake_case (e.g., calculate_free_energy)
     - Variables: snake_case (e.g., belief_state)
     - Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., MAX_AGENTS)
     - Private attributes: leading underscore (e.g., _internal_state)

   - TypeScript/JavaScript:
     - Classes/Components: PascalCase (e.g., AgentCreator)
     - Functions/methods: camelCase (e.g., calculateFreeEnergy)
     - Variables: camelCase (e.g., beliefState)
     - Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., MAX_AGENTS)
     - Private attributes: leading underscore (e.g., _internalState)

4. API endpoint naming:
   - RESTful endpoints: kebab-case (e.g., /api/v1/agent-types)
   - GraphQL queries: camelCase (e.g., getAgentById)

Implementation steps:
1. Create linting rules for each language (flake8, eslint)
2. Write a custom script to rename files according to conventions
3. Use AST manipulation to update variable/function names
4. Add pre-commit hooks to enforce naming conventions
5. Update documentation to reflect naming standards
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Create test cases for each naming convention rule
2. Verify linting catches naming violations
3. Test file renaming script on sample directories
4. Validate that imports are correctly updated after renaming
5. Check that pre-commit hooks properly reject non-compliant names

tasks.title: Implement Active Inference Core
tasks.description: Develop a clean, efficient implementation of Active Inference that serves as the cognitive engine for agents.
tasks.details:
Implement a high-performance Active Inference engine that serves as the cognitive core for all agents:

1. Core components:
   - BeliefState class for representing agent beliefs
   - FreeEnergyCalculator for policy evaluation
   - BeliefUpdater for Bayesian belief updates
   - PolicySelector for action selection

2. Key mathematical implementations:
   - Expected Free Energy calculation: F = -log P(o|s) + KL[Q(s)||P(s)]
   - Belief updates using Bayes rule
   - Precision-weighted policy selection

3. Performance optimizations:
   - Vectorized operations with NumPy
   - JIT compilation with Numba for hot paths
   - Caching for repeated calculations
   - Batch processing for multiple agents

4. Integration points:
   - LLM-informed belief updates
   - GNN (Generalized Notation Notation) integration
   - H3 spatial awareness

Example implementation:

```python
class ActiveInferenceEngine:
    """Core implementation of Active Inference for agent cognition."""
    
    def __init__(self, precision: float = 1.0):
        self.precision = precision  # Controls exploration vs. exploitation
        self._belief_updater = BeliefUpdater()
        self._free_energy_calculator = FreeEnergyCalculator()
    
    def update_beliefs(self, 
                      agent: Agent, 
                      observations: Observations) -> None:
        """Update agent beliefs based on new observations."""
        prior_beliefs = agent.beliefs
        likelihood = self._compute_likelihood(observations)
        
        # Bayesian belief update
        posterior_beliefs = self._belief_updater.update(
            prior_beliefs, 
            likelihood, 
            observations
        )
        
        # Update agent's belief state
        agent.beliefs = posterior_beliefs
    
    def select_policy(self, 
                     agent: Agent, 
                     available_policies: List[Policy]) -> Policy:
        """Select policy that minimizes expected free energy."""
        free_energies = []
        
        for policy in available_policies:
            # Calculate expected free energy for this policy
            ef = self._free_energy_calculator.calculate(
                agent.beliefs, 
                policy, 
                self.precision
            )
            free_energies.append((policy, ef))
        
        # Select policy with minimum free energy
        selected_policy = min(free_energies, key=lambda x: x[1])[0]
        return selected_policy
```

Ensure comprehensive documentation of the mathematical principles and implementation details.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for each component (BeliefUpdater, FreeEnergyCalculator, etc.)
2. Property-based tests to verify mathematical invariants
3. Performance benchmarks comparing optimized vs. naive implementations
4. Integration tests with agent behaviors
5. Validation against published Active Inference papers
6. Edge case testing with extreme belief states

tasks.title: Develop Agent System Architecture
tasks.description: Create a flexible, extensible agent architecture that supports different agent types, behaviors, and coalition formation.
tasks.details:
Design and implement a comprehensive agent system architecture:

1. Base Agent Framework:
   - Abstract BaseAgent class with core interfaces
   - Personality trait system affecting decision-making
   - Belief state management integrated with Active Inference
   - Action selection and execution pipeline

```python
class BaseAgent:
    """Foundation for all agent types in the system."""
    
    def __init__(self, 
                agent_id: str, 
                personality: Dict[str, float] = None):
        self.id = agent_id
        self.personality = personality or {}
        self.beliefs = BeliefState()
        self.active_inference = ActiveInferenceEngine()
        self.location = None  # H3 cell location
        self.energy = 100.0   # Resource management
    
    def perceive(self, observations: Observations) -> None:
        """Process observations and update beliefs."""
        self.active_inference.update_beliefs(self, observations)
    
    def decide(self) -> Policy:
        """Select best policy using Active Inference."""
        available_policies = self._get_available_policies()
        return self.active_inference.select_policy(self, available_policies)
    
    def act(self, world: World) -> ActionResult:
        """Execute selected policy in the world."""
        policy = self.decide()
        action = policy.get_next_action(self.beliefs)
        return world.execute_action(self, action)
    
    def _get_available_policies(self) -> List[Policy]:
        """Get policies available to this agent type."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Subclasses must implement")
```

2. Specialized Agent Types:
   - Explorer: Focuses on discovering resources and patterns
   - Developer: Optimizes and builds systems
   - Merchant: Trades and manages resources
   - Scholar: Analyzes and learns from data

3. Coalition Framework:
   - Coalition formation based on mutual benefit
   - Resource sharing mechanisms
   - Collective decision making
   - Business model implementation

```python
class Coalition:
    """Represents a group of agents working together."""
    
    def __init__(self, coalition_id: str, name: str = None):
        self.id = coalition_id
        self.name = name or f"Coalition-{coalition_id}"
        self.members = set()  # Agent IDs
        self.resources = {}    # Shared resources
        self.business_model = None
        self.contracts = {}    # Agreements between members
    
    def add_member(self, agent: BaseAgent) -> bool:
        """Add agent to coalition if mutually beneficial."""
        # Calculate expected value for agent joining
        agent_benefit = self._calculate_agent_benefit(agent)
        coalition_benefit = self._calculate_coalition_benefit(agent)
        
        # Only add if Pareto improvement (both benefit)
        if agent_benefit > 0 and coalition_benefit > 0:
            self.members.add(agent.id)
            self._create_contract(agent)
            return True
        return False
    
    def operate(self) -> BusinessResults:
        """Run coalition business operations."""
        if not self.business_model:
            raise ValueError("No business model defined")
        
        return self.business_model.execute(self)
```

4. World Integration:
   - H3 spatial grid system
   - Resource distribution
   - Agent interaction mechanics
   - Time and event management

tasks.title: Implement API and Interface Layer
tasks.description: Create a clean, well-documented API for interacting with the agent system, including REST endpoints, WebSocket for real-time updates, and optional GraphQL.
tasks.details:
Design and implement a comprehensive API layer for the agent system:

1. RESTful API using FastAPI:
   - Agent management endpoints
   - Coalition operations
   - Simulation control
   - World state queries

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI(title="FreeAgentics API")

class AgentCreate(BaseModel):
    name: str
    agent_type: str
    personality: dict = {}
    location: Optional[str] = None

class AgentResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    name: str
    agent_type: str
    personality: dict
    location: Optional[str]
    energy: float
    status: str

@app.post("/api/v1/agents", response_model=AgentResponse)
async def create_agent(agent_data: AgentCreate):
    """Create a new agent in the system."""
    try:
        agent = agent_service.create_agent(
            name=agent_data.name,
            agent_type=agent_data.agent_type,
            personality=agent_data.personality,
            location=agent_data.location
        )
        return agent.to_dict()
    except ValueError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))

@app.get("/api/v1/agents/{agent_id}", response_model=AgentResponse)
async def get_agent(agent_id: str):
    """Get agent details by ID."""
    agent = agent_service.get_agent(agent_id)
    if not agent:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Agent not found")
    return agent.to_dict()
```

2. WebSocket for real-time updates:
   - Agent state changes
   - Coalition events
   - World updates

```python
@app.websocket("/ws/world-updates")
async def world_updates_websocket(websocket: WebSocket):
    await websocket.accept()
    try:
        # Subscribe to world events
        subscription_id = event_bus.subscribe("world_updates")
        
        while True:
            # Get updates from event bus
            updates = await event_bus.get_updates(subscription_id)
            
            # Send to client
            if updates:
                await websocket.send_json(updates)
            
            # Small delay to prevent CPU spinning
            await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
    except WebSocketDisconnect:
        # Clean up subscription
        event_bus.unsubscribe(subscription_id)
```

3. GraphQL API (optional):
   - Schema for agents, coalitions, world
   - Queries for complex data retrieval
   - Mutations for system operations
   - Subscriptions for real-time updates

4. API Documentation:
   - OpenAPI specification

tasks.title: Implement Developer Experience Improvements
tasks.description: Enhance the developer experience with intuitive APIs, development tools, debugging capabilities, and comprehensive examples.
tasks.details:
Implement a suite of developer experience improvements:

1. Intuitive API Design:
   - Fluent interface patterns for agent creation
   - Builder patterns for complex objects
   - Sensible defaults with override options
   - Support for both sync and async usage

```python
# Example of fluent interface for agent creation
from freeagentics import Agent, World

# Simple case with sensible defaults
explorer = Agent.create("Explorer")

# Advanced case with fluent interface
developer = (Agent.create("Developer")
    .with_personality(analytical=0.9, efficiency=0.8)
    .with_skills(["optimization", "pattern_recognition"])
    .at_location("8928308280fffff")  # H3 cell
    .with_energy(100)
)

# Coalition creation with builder pattern
coalition = (Coalition.builder()
    .add_agent(explorer)
    .add_agent(developer)
    .with_business_model("ResourceOptimization")
    .with_initial_capital(1000)
    .build()
)

# Async operation with context manager
async with coalition.operate() as business:
    await business.run_until_profitable()
    metrics = business.get_metrics()
    print(f"Revenue: ${metrics.revenue:,.2f}")
```

2. Development Tools:
   - CLI for common operations
   - Development dashboard
   - Test data generators
   - Local development server

```bash
# CLI example
$ freeagentics agent create --name Alice --class Explorer --personality curious:0.9

✨ Created Explorer agent 'Alice' with ID: agent_7f3a9c
📊 Personality: ████████░░ curious (0.9) | ███████░░░ cooperative (0.8)

$ freeagentics world simulate --agents Alice,Bob --steps 1000 --visualize

🌍 Starting world simulation...
⏱️  Step 0/1000: Agents exploring...
🤝 Step 142: Alice and Bob discovered mutual benefit!
💼 Step 156: Coalition 'ResourceMapper Inc' formed!
```

3. Debugging and Observability:
   - OpenTelemetry integration
   - Debug mode with detailed logging
   - Belief state visualizer
   - Performance profiler

```python
# Debugging example
from freeagentics import Agent, enable_tracing
from freeagentics.debug import BeliefVisualizer

# Enable distributed tracing
enable_tracing(endpoint="http://localhost:14268/api/traces")

# Create agent with debug mode
agent = Agent.create("Explorer", debug=True)

# Visualize belief updates in real-time
visualizer = BeliefVisualizer(agent)
visualizer.start()  # Opens browser with live visualization

# Detailed logging of Active Inference calculations
with agent.verbose_logging():
    action = agent.select_action()
```

subtasks.description: Refactor and enhance the API following best practices for resource-based architecture, stateless communication, clear URI design, semantic versioning, and robust security.
subtasks.details: Apply recommendations from Kent Beck, Robert Martin, and Martin Fowler. Ensure APIs use plural nouns for resources, proper HTTP methods, OAuth 2.0/JWT authentication, semantic versioning, and provide OpenAPI/Swagger documentation. Optimize for performance with caching and compression.

subtasks.title: CLI/Tooling Enhancements
subtasks.description: Develop and improve command-line tools and developer utilities to streamline API consumption, testing, and automation.
subtasks.details: Design CLI tools that align with the improved API, supporting common developer workflows such as scaffolding, testing, and deployment. Ensure tools are intuitive and provide helpful error messages and documentation.

subtasks.title: Debugging and Observability
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive debugging and observability features to aid developers in identifying and resolving issues efficiently.
subtasks.details: Integrate structured logging, distributed tracing, and real-time monitoring. Provide clear error reporting, correlation IDs, and metrics dashboards. Ensure observability tools are accessible via both API and CLI.

subtasks.title: Example Suite Development
subtasks.description: Create a robust suite of example projects and code snippets demonstrating best practices and common use cases.
subtasks.details: Develop examples covering authentication, CRUD operations, error handling, and performance optimization. Ensure examples are well-documented, tested, and kept up-to-date with API changes.

subtasks.title: Development Environment Setup
subtasks.description: Standardize and automate the setup of local development environments for contributors and users.
subtasks.details: Provide scripts and configuration files for environment provisioning, dependency management, and consistent tooling. Document setup steps and troubleshooting tips.

subtasks.title: Performance Tools Integration
subtasks.description: Integrate and document performance profiling and benchmarking tools to help developers optimize their workflows.
subtasks.details: Include tools for load testing, latency measurement, and resource usage analysis. Offer guidance on interpreting results and tuning performance.

subtasks.title: User Testing and Feedback Loops
subtasks.description: Establish structured processes for user testing, feedback collection, and iterative improvement.
subtasks.details: Conduct usability studies, surveys, and interviews. Implement feedback mechanisms in CLI and documentation. Regularly review and act on user insights to refine the developer experience.
